mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52% over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress corporate margins and generate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive real policy conditions that suppress equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal yields a negative Rank IC below -0.01 or positive forward returns exceed 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns (t-statistic < 1.5) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that immediately lifts equity prices over a one-week trading horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output during a period of elevated inflation signals supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins and force negative equity repricing over one month.
failure_condition: The negative signal fails to predict 20-day returns with a directional accuracy above 50% during high-inflation regimes.
